name: Feature — Definition of Ready
description: 7-criterion checklist confirming a feature is decomposed and dependency-mapped before its first story enters a sprint.

criteria.name: Capability Articulated in Business Language
criteria.description: The feature's capability is stated in business terms — what a persona can now do — not as an implementation description.
criteria.why: Implementation-led titles ('AUS API Modernization') don't communicate value to the portfolio; capability-led titles do.
criteria.fix: Rewrite the title and capability statement in the form: 'This feature enables [persona] to [do X] so that [outcome].'
criteria.good_example: Real-Time AUS Decision API for Lender Integration — enables Lender Operations users to receive an automated underwriting decision in under 200ms p95.

criteria.name: Linked to Parent Epic
criteria.description: The feature is explicitly linked to a parent epic with a clear contribution to the epic's outcome.
criteria.why: Features without an epic are flagged for portfolio review — they have no strategic anchor.
criteria.fix: Link the feature to its parent epic and state the contribution: '[Feature] advances [Epic outcome] by [specific contribution].'
criteria.good_example: Parent epic: 'Same-Day Lender Commitments for Conforming Loans'. Contribution: enables the AUS decision step that gates same-day commitment.

criteria.name: Persona / User Named
criteria.description: The specific user or persona who consumes this feature is named (e.g. Lender Ops, Asset Manager, Trader, Risk Analyst).
criteria.why: Without a named persona, the squad designs for an abstraction and the feature drifts.
criteria.fix: Name the primary persona using the org's persona library; if no persona exists, define one with the analyst.
criteria.good_example: Primary persona: Lender Operations user (commercial loan desk). Secondary: Underwriting QA reviewer.

criteria.name: 3-5 Acceptance Criteria Written and Agreed
criteria.description: Three to five feature-level acceptance criteria that prove the capability is real — not just code.
criteria.why: Feature-level ACs prevent stories from passing while the feature itself doesn't deliver.
criteria.fix: Write 3-5 binary, testable feature-level ACs and confirm sign-off from the PO and tech lead.
criteria.good_example: AC1: Returns full decision payload < 200ms p95. AC2: Supports 1,000 concurrent lender connections. AC3: Decision logic matches the published rule set 100%.

criteria.name: Story Map Drafted
criteria.description: High-level list of constituent stories (titles + sequence) — proves the feature is decomposable.
criteria.why: Features that can't be decomposed are too big and will drift across multiple sprints unmanaged.
criteria.fix: Draft a story map listing the constituent stories in delivery order; surface any story estimated > 8 points for further split.
criteria.good_example: Story map: 1) AUS request schema · 2) Decision rule engine · 3) Response payload assembly · 4) Lender error handling · 5) End-to-end performance test.

criteria.name: Cross-Team Dependencies Surfaced and Confirmed
criteria.description: Other squads, vendors, and downstream consumers required for the feature are named and have confirmed they're aligned.
criteria.why: Undocumented dependencies create blocking surprises mid-program.
criteria.fix: List upstream dependencies (data feeds, other features, external teams) and downstream consumers; confirm alignment with each named owner.
criteria.good_example: Upstream: EDW team (loan_performance_monthly availability by T+1). Downstream: Risk reporting consumers (weekly slide pack). Confirmed with both leads on 2026-05-01.

criteria.name: Non-Functional Requirements Identified
criteria.description: Performance, security, audit, observability, and compliance requirements are explicitly listed.
criteria.why: NFRs surfaced post-build cause expensive rework, especially in regulated lending workflows.
criteria.fix: List the NFR categories that apply (performance targets, security/audit logging, observability metrics, compliance/data residency) and the specific bar for each.
criteria.good_example: Perf: p95 < 200ms at 1,000 RPS. Security: TLS 1.3, mTLS to lender. Audit: every decision logged with correlation ID. Observability: emit decision_count and decision_latency_ms. Compliance: PII fields encrypted at rest and in transit.
